A NARROW SWATH OF FOCUSED DAMAGE WAS ENCOUNTERED NEAR PALMER AVE AND AN ADJACENT CEMETERY. A POWER POLE WAS SNAPPED AT A NEARBY SOFTBALL FIELD, AND SUBSTANTIAL TREE DAMAGE WAS ENCOUNTERED BEGINNING ALONG PALMER AVE, WHERE HARDWOOD LIMBS WERE SNAPPED, AND SOFTWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ENTIRELY. DAMAGE RESEMBLED THAT OF A QLCS MESOVORTEX TORNADO, BUT THERE WAS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO ARRIVE AT A DECISIVE CAUSE. PEAK WIND WAS ESTIMATED AT 75-80 MPH.

A BRIEF EF0 TORNADO WAS DOCUMENTED FROM ALONG BOWMAN RD TO ALONG BAKER RD. A CONCENTRATED SWATH OF TREE DAMAGE WAS IDENTIFIED ALONG ITS PATH, WITH CONSIDERABLE SNAPPING OF HARDWOOD LIMBS AND SEVERAL HARDWOOD TRUNKS. DAMAGE APPEARS TO HAVE CEASED BEFORE THE STORM CROSSED RT 417. WITNESSES REPORTED OBSERVING A FUNNEL CLOUD AT THE TIME OF THE DAMAGE. THE TIME OF THE TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED PENDING ADDITIONAL INFORMATION. PEAK WIND IS ESTIMATED AT 75-80 MPH.

A BRIEF, WEAK TORNADO OCCURRED ALONG PIONEER RD AND SHREVE RD. DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO SNAPPING OF LARGE BRANCHES FROM MATURE HARDWOOD TREES. A FEW YOUNGER HARDWOOD TREE TRUNKS ALSO WERE SNAPPED. THE TORNADO HAS BEEN RATED EF0 WITH PEAK WIND ESTIMATED AT 65-70 MPH.

SMALL SIGN TRAILER OVER-TURNED ALONG STR 62

AN EF1 TORNADO IN TRUMBULL COUNTY CROSSED INTO MERCER COUNTY BEFORE DISSIPATING, CAUSING HIGH-END EF0 DAMAGE PRIMARILY TO TREES. A PENNDOT TRAILER WAS TIPPED OVER, TREES WERE TOPPED, AND SOME WERE SNAPPED IN SHARON AND FARRELL. MOST TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG MALLEABLE STREET, AND SOUTH OAKLAND AVENUE NEAR GEORGE AND MORRISON STREETS. ALTHOUGH DAMAGE IN MERCER COUNTY WAS CONSISTENT WITH EF0 WIND UP TO 80 MPH, THE TORNADO WILL BE RATED EF1 BASED ON DAMAGE IN TRUMBULL COUNTY.

A HIGH-WATER RESCUE WAS CONDUCTED ALONG SCHOOL HOUSE ROAD. ADDITIONAL FLOODING WAS EXPERIENCED ALONG MOORE ROAD AND HEDGES LANE. SCHOOL HOUSE ROAD AND MOORE ROAD REMAINED CLOSED AS OF NOON THE FOLLOWING DAY.

NEAR SIX INCHES OF RAIN OBSERVED IN THE AREA LED TO FLASH FLOODING ALONG WAKATOMIKA CREEK IN SEVERAL AREAS. CULVERTS WERE WASHED OUT ALONG HAMBY HILL ROAD, AND PAVEMENT AND A SMALL BRIDGE WERE DAMAGED ALONG PIKE ROAD. THE CREEK GAUGE SAW A NEAR 7 FT RISE IN RESPONSE TO THE HEAVY RAIN.
